Its why toy guns get confiscated in airports, even if they don't look anything like real guns.  Its why poking someone in the back with a sharpie and telling them you've got a gun will get you in just as much trouble as if you used a real gun.  The weapon does not need to be real to qualify as a weapon in a terror attack.
